epic.name: Incident REST API
epic.objective: Implement REST API endpoints for incident CRUD operations and status transitions
epic.definition_of_done: All v0.1 incident endpoints functional: list, get, create (manual), update (acknowledge/resolve), with proper validation and error responses

tasks.name: Implement GET /api/v1/incidents endpoint
tasks.description: List incidents with filtering and pagination
acceptance_criteria: Returns paginated list of incidents
Query params: status, severity, created_after, created_before, limit (default 50, max 250), offset
Response includes: incidents array, total count, limit, offset
Incidents ordered by created_at DESC by default
Each incident includes: id, incident_number, title, status, severity, slack_channel_name, created_at, acknowledged_at, resolved_at

tasks.name: Implement GET /api/v1/incidents/:id endpoint
tasks.description: Get single incident with full details including timeline and linked alerts
acceptance_criteria: Returns incident by UUID or incident_number (detect format)
Response includes all incident fields
Includes linked alerts array
Includes timeline entries (last 50 by default)
Includes slack_channel object with id, name, url
Returns 404 if incident not found

tasks.name: Implement POST /api/v1/incidents endpoint
acceptance_criteria: Request body: title (required), severity, description
Creates incident with created_by_type='user'
Generates slug from title
Triggers Slack channel creation
Returns created incident with 201 status
Timeline entry: type=incident_created, content={trigger: 'manual'}

tasks.name: Implement PATCH /api/v1/incidents/:id endpoint
tasks.description: Update incident status, severity, or summary
acceptance_criteria: Request body: status, severity, summary (all optional)
Status transitions validated: triggered->acknowledged->resolved, triggered->resolved, triggered->canceled
acknowledged_at set when status changes to acknowledged
resolved_at set when status changes to resolved
Timeline entry created for each field change
Returns updated incident
Returns 400 for invalid status transitions

tasks.name: Implement GET /api/v1/incidents/:id/timeline endpoint
tasks.description: Get timeline entries for an incident
acceptance_criteria: Returns paginated timeline entries
Query params: types (filter), limit, offset
Entries ordered by timestamp ASC
Each entry includes: id, timestamp, type, actor_type, actor_id, content

tasks.name: Implement POST /api/v1/incidents/:id/timeline endpoint
tasks.description: Add manual timeline entry (e.g., user note)
acceptance_criteria: Request body: type, content
Only allows type='message' for manual entries
Sets actor_type='user', actor_id from auth context (or 'anonymous' for v0.1)
Returns created entry with 201 status
Timestamp is server-generated

tasks.name: Implement Slack status update notifications
tasks.description: Post message to incident Slack channel when status changes
acceptance_criteria: When incident status changes, message posted to Slack channel
Message includes: new status, who changed it, timestamp
Message threaded under original incident message if possible
Failure to post does not fail the status update

tasks.name: Implement API error handling and response format
tasks.description: Standardize error responses across all endpoints
acceptance_criteria: All errors return JSON: {error: {code: string, message: string, details: object}}
400 Bad Request for validation errors with field-level details
404 Not Found with resource type in message
500 Internal Server Error logs full error, returns safe message
Request ID included in all responses for tracing
